narration: Reversal starts by saving the rest of the list before flipping any arrow.
callout: Current is at 1, prev is null, and nextNode should save 2.
decision: Save nextNode first so the forward path is not lost.

narration: After one flip, 1 points backward to null and prev moves up to 1.
callout: Current then moves to the saved nextNode, which is 2.
decision: Every step saves, flips, and moves.

predictionPrompt.prompt: Why must nextNode be saved before changing current.next?

choices.label: To protect the rest of the list
choices.feedback: Correct. Without nextNode, the forward chain could be lost.

choices.label: To make the code run faster
choices.feedback: Not this one. The key reason is safety, not speed.

narration: When every arrow has flipped, prev points at the new head 3.
callout: The reversed chain now flows 3 to 2 to 1 to null.

reflectionPrompt.prompt: Why is prev the new head after reversal finishes?
reflectionPrompt.placeholder: Prev becomes the new head because...
